query: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visit to Cuba May 2026 details and any follow-up visits by US cabinet officials planned
answer:
I've completed the research on CIA Director John Ratcliffe's May 2026 Cuba visit and follow-up cabinet-official plans. Here's the key takeaway:

**The Visit:** Ratcliffe visited Havana on **May 14, 2026**, meeting Raúl Rodríguez Castro (grandson of Raúl Castro), Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and Cuba's intelligence chief. He delivered Trump's message offering economic/security engagement (including $100M aid and Starlink) contingent on Cuba making "fundamental changes," amid Cuba's fuel/power crisis.

**Follow-up by cabinet officials:** There is **no publicly documented, formally scheduled cabinet-level follow-up visit** to Cuba. The only confirmed post-Ratcliffe U.S. engagement was a **general-level** (not cabinet) military meeting near Guantánamo Bay on **May 29–30, 2026**, led by SOUTHCOM chief Gen. Francis Donovan. By May 22, Rubio (who was not on the Havana delegation) had publicly voiced skepticism about diplomacy, and the administration pivoted to sanctions and even a Trump military-action threat rather than announcing further high-level visits.

The full synthesis, sources, key facts, and structured data points have been provided via the StructuredOutput tool.

key_facts: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Havana, Cuba on Thursday, May 14, 2026 (confirmed by Cuba May 15).
Ratcliffe met Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro (grandson of Raúl Castro), Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and Cuba's intelligence chief.
Topics: intelligence cooperation, economic stability, and security; U.S. demanded Cuba stop harboring U.S. adversaries.
Ratcliffe delivered Trump's message: U.S. will engage economically/security-wise only if Cuba makes 'fundamental changes,' with enforceable 'red lines.'
Visit occurred amid Cuba's power-grid collapse and fuel reserves hitting zero under U.S. sanctions.
U.S. offered $100 million in humanitarian aid (via Catholic Church/independent groups) and Starlink internet restoration.
It was the first U.S. government plane in Havana since Obama's 2016 visit.
Prior 2026 engagements: Rubio met Rodríguez Castro secretly in St. Kitts (Feb); State officials Jeremy Lewin and Michael Kozak led an April 10 Havana delegation.
Post-visit: DOJ began indictment prep against Raúl Castro (May 15); new sanctions imposed (May 18); Rubio voiced doubt on diplomacy and Trump threatened military action (May 22).

Follow-up: SOUTHCOM chief Gen. Francis Donovan met Cuban military officials near Guantánamo Bay on May 29–30, 2026 — first such SOUTHCOM-chief meeting in recent memory; both sides called it 'positive.'
No publicly documented, formally scheduled cabinet-level follow-up visit to Cuba as of late May 2026; Rubio was not part of the Havana delegation.
The Guantánamo military meeting was led by a general, not a cabinet official; U.S. side reportedly 'unimpressed' and pivoted to sanctions.
